close_reason: Positive observation: '..' rejection at construction is correct
description: lib.rs:62-68 rejects any path with a Component::ParentDir. The module doc (lines 8-14) and README (line 36) honestly state this is defense-in-depth, not a complete sandbox: 'paths containing .. are rejected at construction time, but that alone is not sufficient if the path root itself is attacker-controlled'. A future contributor might (a) try to canonicalize() the path (introduces TOCTOU, fails on missing files, follows symlinks — all worse), or (b) remove the check claiming it adds no security (true in absolute terms but the layered defense catches a real class of bug). Keep both the check and the honest docs.
title: what is good: rejecting '..' components at construction time is the right defense-in-depth posture, with honest docs
